# Swap.Htmx — LLM Playbook

Use this as the "golden path" when generating Swap.Htmx code.
Prefer correctness + consistency over cleverness.

================================================================================
## Quick Start (Template)
================================================================================

```bash
# Install templates
dotnet new install Swap.Templates

# Create a modular monolith project (recommended)
dotnet new swap-modular -n MyApp

# Or create a simple MVC project
dotnet new swap-mvc -n MyApp
```

================================================================================
## 0. Non-Negotiables
================================================================================

1. Controllers: inherit `SwapController`.
   - Realtime SSE endpoints: inherit `SwapRealtimeController` (from `Swap.Htmx.Realtime`).
2. Views: return `SwapView()` (not `View()`).
3. Multi-target updates: return `SwapResponse()` + `.AlsoUpdate(...)` + `.Build()`.
4. No magic strings:
   - Views: use generated `SwapViews.*` constants.
   - Element IDs: use generated `SwapElements.*` constants.
   - Events: use generated `EventKey` constants via `[SwapEventSource]`.
5. State/paging/filtering: use `<swap-state>` + `[FromSwapState]`.
6. Decoupling: use `ISwapEventConfiguration` for event chains and/or `[SwapHandler]` handlers.
7. Protected state (`Protected => true`) requires ASP.NET Core Data Protection — `AddSwapHtmx()` registers it, and rendering protected state without a provider throws (never leaks plaintext). Tamper protection **fails closed**: a tampered/empty protected value fails model binding, so check `ModelState.IsValid` (or `SwapState.Tampered`) and handle it explicitly — it no longer silently becomes the type default.
8. Redirect/navigation URLs (`WithRedirect()`, `WithNavigation()`) must be `http`/`https` absolute or same-origin relative (allowlist). Protocol-relative (`//host`, `/\host`) and non-http(s) schemes (`javascript:`, `data:`, `vbscript:`, …) throw `ArgumentException`.

================================================================================
## 1. Minimal Setup (Program.cs)
================================================================================

### Services

```csharp
builder.Services.AddControllersWithViews();

builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    // Auto-suppress layout for HTMX requests
    options.AutoSuppressLayout = true;
    
    // Default navigation target for <swap-nav>
    options.DefaultNavigationTarget = "#main-content";
    
    // Register modular event configurations (recommended)
    options.AddConfig<TaskEventConfig>();
    options.AddConfig<ProjectEventConfig>();
    
    // Or configure event chains inline
    // options.EventBus.When(TaskEvents.Task.Completed)
    //     .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, ctx => GetTasks())
    //     .Toast("Task completed!", ToastType.Success);
});

// Optional: Enable realtime (SSE) broadcasting
builder.Services.AddSseEventBridge();
```

### Middleware Pipeline

```csharp
app.UseStaticFiles();
app.UseRouting();
app.UseSwapHtmx();
app.UseSseEventBridge();  // Optional: requires AddSseEventBridge()
app.MapControllers();
```

================================================================================
## 2. Layout Requirements (_Layout.cshtml)
================================================================================

Minimum for HTMX + Swap client:

```html
<head>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/css/swap.css" />
</head>
<body>
    <div id="main-content">
        @RenderBody()
    </div>
    
    <script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2.0.8"></script>
    <script src="~/_content/Swap.Htmx/js/swap.client.js"></script>
    
    <!-- If using SSE realtime -->
    <script src="https://unpkg.com/htmx-ext-sse@2.2.4/sse.js"></script>
</body>
```

================================================================================
## 3. Core Patterns
================================================================================

### 3.1 SwapController & SwapView

Every controller should inherit from `SwapController`:

```csharp
public class TasksController : SwapController
{
    [HttpGet("/tasks")]
    public IActionResult Index()
    {
        var tasks = _db.Tasks.ToList();
        return SwapView(tasks);
        // Browser navigation → Full view with layout
        // HTMX request → Partial view (no layout)
    }
    
    [HttpGet("/tasks/{id}")]
    public IActionResult Details(int id)
    {
        var task = _db.Tasks.Find(id);
        return SwapView(SwapViews.Tasks.Details, task);
    }
}
```

### 3.2 SwapResponse (Multi-Target Updates)

Update multiple page elements in one response:

```csharp
[HttpPost("/tasks")]
public IActionResult Create(CreateTaskInput input)
{
    var task = _service.Create(input);
    
    return SwapResponse()
        .WithView(SwapViews.Tasks._TaskRow, task)          // Main response → hx-target
        .AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.TaskCount, SwapViews.Tasks._Count, _db.Tasks.Count())
        .AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.Stats, SwapViews.Dashboard._Stats, GetStats())
        .WithCreatedToast("Task")                          // "Task created successfully"
        .WithTrigger(TaskEvents.Task.Created)              // Client-side event
        .WithClientAction("closeModal")                    // Close modal
        .Build();
}
```

### AlsoUpdate Parameters

```csharp
.AlsoUpdate(
    "element-id",        // Target element ID (no # prefix, validated)
    "_PartialView",      // Partial view name
    model,               // Model for the view
    SwapMode.OuterHTML   // Optional: swap strategy (default OuterHTML)
)
```

### SwapMode Options

| Mode | Effect |
|------|--------|
| `OuterHTML` | Replace entire element (default) |
| `InnerHTML` | Replace inner content only |
| `MorphOuter` | Morph element + children in place (idiomorph) — keeps focus/caret/scroll |
| `MorphInner` | Morph only inner content in place (idiomorph) |
| `BeforeBegin` | Insert before element |
| `AfterBegin` | Insert at start of element's children |
| `BeforeEnd` | Append to element's children |
| `AfterEnd` | Insert after element |
| `Delete` | Remove element |
| `None` | No swap |

`MorphInner`/`MorphOuter` require the idiomorph extension — see §3.5 and `<swap-scripts morph="true">` (§8).

### OOB Target ID Rules

Target IDs are validated at build time. Valid IDs must:
- Start with a letter (`a-z`, `A-Z`)
- Contain only letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores
- Not be empty or whitespace

A leading `#` is automatically stripped. Invalid IDs throw `ArgumentException`.

### OOB Rendering

All OOB swaps in a response are rendered **sequentially**, in registration order. This applies to MVC (`SwapActionResult`), Minimal API (`SwapResult`), and Razor Pages (`SwapPageResult`). Every partial renders on the single request scope (scoped `DbContext`, `ViewData`, the shared view-buffer pool), so sequential rendering avoids racing those scoped services; rendering is CPU-bound string building, so the cost is negligible. (Reverts the 1.3.0 `Task.WhenAll` parallelization, which intermittently threw "A second operation was started on this context instance".)

### OOB Coalescing

Before rendering, duplicate OOB swaps to the **same target id** are coalesced so the client never double-swaps:
- **Replace-style** modes (`OuterHTML`, `InnerHTML`, `MorphOuter`, `MorphInner`, `Delete`, `None`) collapse to the **last** one queued for that target (earlier ones dropped — the last would overwrite them anyway).
- **Insert-style** modes (`BeforeBegin`, `AfterBegin`, `BeforeEnd`, `AfterEnd`) **accumulate** — every one survives (appending N rows keeps all N).
- Surviving order is preserved. This is a safety net (e.g. a handler's `cart-total` refresh on top of the controller's won't double-render); don't queue duplicates on purpose. Related: an explicit `AlsoUpdate` for a target makes the dependency graph (§3.3) skip that fragment.

### Conditional OOB Swaps

```csharp
// Only include if condition is true (server-side check)
.AlsoUpdateIf(user.HasPermission("ViewStats"), SwapElements.Stats, "_Stats", stats)

// Only swap if element exists in DOM (client-side check)
.AlsoUpdateIfExists(SwapElements.OptionalPanel, "_Panel", data)
```

### SwapResponseBuilder Full API

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `.WithView(view, model)` | Main response content |
| `.AlsoUpdate(id, view, model, mode?, seq?, fingerprint?)` | OOB swap (optional `seq:` version stamp + `fingerprint:` diff-skip — see §3.4) |
| `.AlsoMorph(id, view, model, innerHtml?, seq?, fingerprint?)` | OOB morph (idiomorph; keeps focus/caret/scroll) — see §3.5 |
| `.AlsoUpdateMany(items, idSelector, view)` | Batch OOB swaps |
| `.AlsoUpdateIf(condition, id, view, model)` | Conditional OOB (server-side check) |
| `.AlsoUpdateIfExists(id, view, model)` | OOB only if DOM element exists (client-enforced — see §3.4) |
| `.Invalidate(params topics)` | Re-render dependency-graph fragments for changed topics — see §3.3 |
| `.WithSuccessToast(msg)` | Success toast |
| `.WithErrorToast(msg)` | Error toast |
| `.WithWarningToast(msg)` | Warning toast |
| `.WithInfoToast(msg)` | Info toast |
| `.WithFlash(msg, type?)` | Toast that survives an htmx navigation — see §3.6 |
| `.WithCreatedToast(entity)` | "Entity created successfully" |
| `.WithUpdatedToast(entity)` | "Entity updated" |
| `.WithDeletedToast(entity)` | "Entity deleted" |
| `.WithSavedToast(entity)` | "Entity saved" |
| `.WithTrigger(eventKey, payload?)` | Client-side HX-Trigger event |
| `.WithRedirect(url)` | HX-Redirect (allowlist: http/https or same-origin relative only) |
| `.WithNavigation(path, target?, pushUrl?)` | HX-Location (allowlist: http/https or same-origin relative only) |
| `.WithClientAction(action, target?, value?)` | Client action (focus, reset, closeModal) |
| `.WithState(state)` | OOB state container update |
| `.Build()` | Build IActionResult |
| `.BuildResult()` | Build IResult (Minimal APIs) |

### 3.3 Fragment Dependency Graph (Invalidate) — PREFERRED for coordinated dashboards

Register dependency-aware fragments ONCE at startup, then invalidate a topic instead of
hand-listing every dependent widget on every action.

```csharp
// Program.cs — startup only (the registry FREEZES after AddSwapHtmx; registering at
// request time throws). Duplicate ids throw. Resolve scoped services from ctx.RequestServices.
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(o =>
{
    o.Fragments.Fragment("revenue", "_Revenue",
            ctx => ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<IStats>().Revenue())
        .DependsOn("orders", "refunds");                 // topics that re-render this fragment
    o.Fragments.Fragment("order-count", "_OrderCount",
            ctx => ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<IStats>().OrderCount())
        .DependsOn("orders");
});
```

```csharp
// Any action — say WHAT changed; the engine re-renders every dependent fragment as OOB swaps.
return SwapResponse()
    .WithView("_OrderRow", order)
    .Invalidate("orders")       // → revenue + order-count refresh, deduplicated, in registration order
    .Build();
```

- Signature: `Fragment(string id, string viewName, Func<HttpContext, object?> modelFactory, SwapMode swapMode = SwapMode.OuterHTML)` → `.DependsOn(params string[] topics)`.
- Each fragment renders at most ONCE per response, even if multiple invalidated topics hit it.
- Explicit `AlsoUpdate`/`AlsoMorph` for the same target id WINS — the graph skips that fragment (coalescing).
- Fragments carry NO `data-swap-seq` (they re-render from authoritative current state). Works in MVC / Minimal API / Razor Pages.

### 3.4 Client OOB Guards (`seq:` / `fingerprint:` / if-exists)

The Swap client inspects each incoming OOB swap in `htmx:oobBeforeSwap` and can drop it. Three independent, opt-in guards (evaluated existence → seq → hash). ALL require `<swap-scripts>` (the guards live in the Swap client); a swap carrying none of the stamps is never touched.

**Version (`seq:`)** — drop stale/duplicate updates (matters for realtime out-of-order/replayed broadcasts):

```csharp
// seq = a value that only increases for this element (rowversion, sequence number, long timestamp)
.AlsoUpdate("order-42", "_OrderRow", order, seq: order.Version)
.AlsoMorph("order-42", "_OrderRow", order, seq: order.Version)
```
Renders `data-swap-seq="N"`; the client tracks the highest applied seq per target id and cancels any swap where `seq <= previous`.

**Content diff-skip (`fingerprint: true`)** — skip the swap when the new content is identical to what's on the target (no re-render, no lost focus/scroll). Use for fragments that refresh often but rarely change (dashboard tiles, mostly-unchanged SSE fan-out):

```csharp
.AlsoUpdate("revenue", "_Revenue", stats, fingerprint: true)
.AlsoMorph("order-count", "_OrderCount", stats, fingerprint: true)
```
Server hashes the rendered fragment (stable FNV-1a) → `data-swap-hash="…"`; the client skips when the incoming hash matches the hash already on the target. The FIRST fingerprinted swap always applies (nothing to compare yet) and stamps the target, so the next identical render is what gets skipped.

**Existence (`AlsoUpdateIfExists`)** — client-enforced guarded swap for an optional region that may not be on the page:

```csharp
.AlsoUpdateIfExists("sidebar-stats", "_Stats", stats)   // emits data-swap-if-exists="true"
```
If the target id isn't in the DOM when the response lands, the client skips it cleanly — no stray detached element, no htmx no-target error. (Plain `AlsoUpdate` to a missing target is unaffected.)

### 3.5 DOM Morphing (idiomorph)

Patch a node in place — keeps focus, caret, scroll position, in-flight CSS transitions — instead of replacing it.

```csharp
.AlsoMorph("filter-panel", "_FilterPanel", state)                 // morph:outerHTML (whole element)
.AlsoMorph("results", "_Results", results, innerHtml: true)       // morph:innerHTML (children only)
// equivalently: .AlsoUpdate("filter-panel", "_FilterPanel", state, SwapMode.MorphOuter)
```

- REQUIRES the idiomorph extension: `<swap-scripts morph="true" />` AND `hx-ext="morph"` on `<body>` (§8).
- Use for live-filtered lists / search-as-you-type / frequently-refreshed dashboard regions with focus/scroll to preserve.

### 3.6 Flash Toasts (survive an htmx navigation)

`WithFlash(message, type?)` — show a message AFTER an htmx-driven navigation (save → navigate → confirm).

```csharp
return SwapResponse()
    .WithFlash("Profile saved", ToastType.Success)
    .WithNavigation("/profile")   // htmx-issued HX-Location follow-up → flash shows on arrival
    .Build();
```

- Stashed in TempData (MVC/Razor Pages), re-emitted as `HX-Trigger showToast` on the next response;
  Minimal APIs have no TempData so it emits immediately. Requires the Swap client (`<swap-scripts>`).
- IMPORTANT: fires only on an htmx-issued follow-up — a boosted nav or `WithNavigation` (HX-Location).
  It does NOT surface after a full-page `WithRedirect` (HX-Redirect) reload, because htmx only reads the
  `HX-Trigger` header on requests it issues itself. Don't pair `WithFlash` with `WithRedirect`.

### 3.7 Multi-Step Flows (SwapFlow)

Server-authoritative wizard/step-machine (namespace `Swap.Htmx.Flows`). Pure — no HTTP/SwapState dependency; YOU persist `CurrentIndex`.

```csharp
public class CheckoutFlow : SwapFlow
{
    protected override IReadOnlyList<SwapFlowStep> Steps => new[]
    {
        new SwapFlowStep("cart",     "_Cart"),
        new SwapFlowStep("shipping", "_Shipping", CanEnter: () => _cart.HasItems),  // guard
        new SwapFlowStep("payment",  "_Payment"),
    };
}
```

- Navigation: `Next()`/`GoTo(i)` honour the target step's `CanEnter` guard (return false if blocked);
  `Previous()` is unguarded; `RestoreIndex(i)` clamps + ignores guards (rehydrate persisted position).
- State: `CurrentIndex`, `Current`, `CanGoNext`, `CanGoPrevious`. Persist `CurrentIndex` via `<swap-state>`
  (§4), `RestoreIndex` it on the way in, save it after navigating. Register the flow: `AddScoped<CheckoutFlow>()`.

### 3.8 Optimistic UI (`data-swap-optimistic`)

Client-runtime safety net: snapshot a region before the request, roll it back on failure. YOU apply the instant visual change; the library only guarantees a failed request undoes it. Purely a client attribute — no server API.

```html
<button hx-post="/todos/42/complete" hx-target="#todo-42" hx-swap="outerHTML"
        data-swap-optimistic="#todo-42"
        hx-on:click="this.closest('#todo-42').classList.add('done')">
    Mark done
</button>
```

- On `htmx:beforeRequest`: snapshot the target's `innerHTML`, add `swap-pending` class to the requesting element (style it: spinner/disabled).
- Value is a CSS selector for the region to snapshot; empty value → falls back to `hx-target`, else the element itself.
- On SUCCESS: htmx swaps in the authoritative server response; snapshot discarded.
- On FAILURE (`detail.successful === false` — non-2xx / network error / timeout): restore the snapshot and re-run `htmx.process` on it (rehydrate `hx-*`). `swap-pending` is removed either way.
- REQUIRES `<swap-scripts>`. Snapshot is the region's own pre-request server-rendered HTML (trusted, not external input).

================================================================================
## 4. SwapState (Server-Side UI State)
================================================================================

### Define State

```csharp
public class TaskFilterState : SwapState
{
    // Optional: Encrypt state values (tamper-proof)
    public override bool Protected => true;
    
    // Optional: Sync state to URL query string (bookmarkable)
    public override bool UrlSync => true;

    // User-editable fields (exempt from encryption)
    [SwapUnprotected]
    public string Status { get; set; } = "all";
    
    [SwapUnprotected]
    public int Page { get; set; } = 1;
    
    [SwapUnprotected]
    public string? Search { get; set; }
    
    // Protected field (cannot be tampered)
    public int ProjectId { get; set; }
}
```

### Render State in View

```html
@model TaskListViewModel

<!-- Renders hidden inputs in a container -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Or with explicit ID -->
<swap-state state="Model.State" id="custom-state-id" />
```

### Include State in HTMX Requests

```html
<button hx-get="/tasks?Status=completed&Page=1"
        hx-target="#task-list"
        hx-include="#task-filter-state">
    Completed Tasks
</button>

<!-- Search with debounce -->
<input type="text" 
       name="Search"
       value="@Model.State.Search"
       hx-get="/tasks?Page=1"
       hx-target="#task-list"
       hx-include="#task-filter-state"
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms" />
```

### Bind State in Controller

```csharp
[HttpGet("/tasks")]
public IActionResult Index([FromSwapState] TaskFilterState state)
{
    // state.Status = "completed" (from URL param, overrides hidden field)
    // state.Page = 1 (from URL param)
    // state.Search = "review" (from hidden field or input)
    
    var tasks = _service.GetTasks(state.Status, state.Search, state.Page);
    return SwapView(new TaskListViewModel { State = state, Tasks = tasks });
}
```

### CRITICAL: URL Parameters Win (First Value)

When using `hx-get` with URL params AND `hx-include`:
- Request becomes: `?Status=completed&Page=1&Status=all&Page=5&...`
- Model binder uses the FIRST value
- URL params override hidden fields

Pattern:
- **Override specific fields:** Put them in URL parameters
- **Preserve other fields:** They come from hidden fields via hx-include
- **Reset a field:** Pass empty value in URL (`Search=`)

### Checkbox Toggle Pattern

Unchecked checkboxes send nothing. Toggle explicitly:

```html
<input type="checkbox" 
       @(state.IncludeArchived ? "checked" : "")
       hx-get="/tasks?IncludeArchived=@(!state.IncludeArchived)"
       hx-target="#task-list"
       hx-include="#task-filter-state" />
```

### Protected State Links

For `Protected` state, you cannot manually build query strings. Use the helper:

```html
<a href="/tasks?@Html.SwapStateQueryString(Model.State)">
    Bookmarkable Link
</a>
```

### Protected State Fails Closed

Protected state requires `IDataProtectionProvider` (registered by `AddSwapHtmx()`); rendering it without a provider throws instead of emitting plaintext. On bind, a protected value that is present-but-empty or fails to decrypt is a hard failure — the binder adds a `ModelState` error and sets `SwapState.Tampered = true` (it no longer silently defaults to `0`/`Guid.Empty`). Always verify before trusting protected state:

```csharp
public IActionResult Checkout([FromSwapState] PaymentState state)
{
    if (!ModelState.IsValid || state.Tampered)
        return SwapResponse().WithErrorToast("Invalid request.").Build();
    // state is trustworthy here
}
```

================================================================================
## 5. Source Generators
================================================================================

### 5.1 [SwapEventSource] — Type-Safe Events

Define events as string constants, generator creates `EventKey` properties:

```csharp
using Swap.Htmx.Attributes;

[SwapEventSource]
public partial class TaskEvents
{
    public const string TaskCreated = "task.created";
    public const string TaskCompleted = "task.completed";
    public const string TaskDeleted = "task.deleted";
}

// Generated at build time:
// TaskEvents.Task.Created  → EventKey("task.created")
// TaskEvents.Task.Completed → EventKey("task.completed")
// TaskEvents.Task.Deleted   → EventKey("task.deleted")

// Usage — type-safe, refactorable, no typos
return SwapEvent(TaskEvents.Task.Completed, payload).Build();
```

### 5.2 SwapViews (Auto-Generated)

Views are auto-scanned and grouped by controller folder:

```csharp
// Auto-generated from Views/**/*.cshtml
public static class SwapViews
{
    public static class Tasks
    {
        public const string Index = "Index";
        public const string Details = "Details";
        public const string _List = "_List";           // Partials keep underscore
        public const string _TaskRow = "_TaskRow";
    }
    public static class Dashboard
    {
        public const string Index = "Index";
        public const string _Stats = "_Stats";
    }
}

// Usage — controller-relative, no magic strings
return SwapView(SwapViews.Tasks.Index, model);
builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, tasks);
```

### 5.3 SwapElements (Auto-Generated)

Element IDs are auto-scanned from `id="..."` attributes:

```html
<!-- Views/Tasks/_List.cshtml -->
<div id="task-list">...</div>
<div id="task-count">...</div>
```

```csharp
// Auto-generated (filtered for meaningful IDs)
public static class SwapElements
{
    public const string TaskList = "task-list";
    public const string TaskCount = "task-count";
}

// Usage
builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, tasks);
```

### Dynamic Element IDs

For per-item IDs, combine constants with interpolation:

```csharp
public static class TaskElements
{
    public const string RowPrefix = "task-row-";
    public static string Row(int id) => $"task-row-{id}";
}

// Update specific row
builder.AlsoUpdate(TaskElements.Row(taskId), SwapViews.Tasks._TaskRow, task);
```

================================================================================
## 6. Event System
================================================================================

### 6.1 ISwapEventConfiguration (Modular Event Chains)

Organize event configuration by feature:

```csharp
public class TaskEventConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions config)
    {
        // When task.created fires, refresh these partials
        config.When(TaskEvents.Task.Created)
            .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, ctx =>
            {
                var db = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>();
                return db.Tasks.OrderByDescending(t => t.CreatedAt).ToList();
            })
            .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.Stats, SwapViews.Dashboard._Stats, ctx =>
            {
                var db = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>();
                return new StatsModel { TaskCount = db.Tasks.Count() };
            })
            .Toast("Task created!", ToastType.Success);

        // Chain to SSE for realtime updates
        config.ChainToSse(TaskEvents.Task.Created, SseEvents.Subscribers("task.created"));
    }
}
```

Register in Program.cs:

```csharp
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    options.AddConfig<TaskEventConfig>();
    options.AddConfig<ProjectEventConfig>();
});
```

### 6.2 SwapEvent / SwapEventAsync

Fire events from controllers:

```csharp
[HttpPost("/tasks/{id}/complete")]
public IActionResult Complete(int id)
{
    var task = _service.Complete(id);
    
    // Synchronous: all RefreshPartial model factories run synchronously
    return SwapEvent(TaskEvents.Task.Completed, new TaskPayload(task))
        .WithView(SwapViews.Tasks._TaskRow, task)
        .Build();
}

[HttpPost("/tasks")]
public async Task<IActionResult> Create(CreateTaskInput input)
{
    var task = await _service.CreateAsync(input);
    
    // Async: use when event chains have async model factories
    return (await SwapEventAsync(TaskEvents.Task.Created, new TaskPayload(task)))
        .WithClientAction("closeModal")
        .Build();
}
```

### 6.3 Distributed Handlers ([SwapHandler])

For cross-cutting concerns without bloating controllers:

```csharp
[SwapHandler(Priority = 100)]
public class TaskAuditHandler : ISwapEventHandler<TaskPayload>
{
    private readonly IAuditService _audit;
    
    public TaskAuditHandler(IAuditService audit)
    {
        _audit = audit;
    }
    
    public async Task HandleAsync(TaskPayload payload, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        await _audit.LogAsync($"Task {payload.TaskId} action performed");
        
        // Optionally add more OOB swaps
        builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.AuditLog, SwapViews.Shared._AuditEntry, payload);
    }
}

// Handler implementing multiple event types
[SwapHandler]
public class DashboardRefreshHandler : 
    ISwapEventHandler<TaskPayload>,
    ISwapEventHandler<ProjectPayload>
{
    public Task HandleAsync(TaskPayload payload, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.DashboardStats, "_Stats", GetStats());
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
    
    public Task HandleAsync(ProjectPayload payload, SwapResponseBuilder builder, CancellationToken ct)
    {
        builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.DashboardStats, "_Stats", GetStats());
        return Task.CompletedTask;
    }
}
```

================================================================================
## 7. Realtime (SSE)
================================================================================

### 7.1 Setup

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options => { /* ... */ });
builder.Services.AddSseEventBridge();  // Enable SSE

// Middleware
app.UseSwapHtmx();
app.UseSseEventBridge();
```

### 7.2 SwapRealtimeController

Create SSE endpoints by inheriting `SwapRealtimeController`:

```csharp
public class DashboardController : SwapRealtimeController
{
    [HttpGet("/dashboard/stream")]
    public IActionResult Stream()
    {
        return ServerSentEvents(async (conn, ct) =>
        {
            // Subscribe to specific events
            conn.WithEvents("task.created", "task.completed");
            
            // Or join a room
            conn.JoinRoom("project-123");
            
            // Keep connection alive
            await conn.KeepAlive(cancellationToken: ct);
        });
    }
}
```

### 7.3 Connect from View

```html
<div hx-ext="sse" sse-connect="/dashboard/stream">
    <!-- This div updates when "task.created" event arrives -->
    <div id="task-list" sse-swap="task.created" hx-swap="innerHTML">
        @await Html.PartialAsync("_TaskList", Model.Tasks)
    </div>
    
    <!-- This div updates when "activity.logged" event arrives -->
    <div id="activity-feed" sse-swap="activity.logged" hx-swap="afterbegin">
        @await Html.PartialAsync("_ActivityFeed", Model.Activities)
    </div>
</div>
```

### 7.4 Chain Domain Events to SSE

In your event configuration:

```csharp
public class TaskEventConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions config)
    {
        // Forward task.created to SSE subscribers
        config.ChainToSse(TaskEvents.Task.Created, SseEvents.Subscribers("task.created"));
        
        // Forward to a specific room
        config.ChainToSse(TaskEvents.Task.Updated, SseEvents.Room("project-123", "task.updated"));
        
        // Forward to all connected clients
        config.ChainToSse(TaskEvents.Task.Deleted, SseEvents.Broadcast("task.deleted"));
    }
}
```

### 7.5 SseEvents Helper

```csharp
SseEvents.Broadcast("event-name")           // All connections
SseEvents.Subscribers("event-name")         // Connections subscribed to this event
SseEvents.Room("room-id", "event-name")     // Connections in a room
SseEvents.Rooms("room1", "room2").Send("event-name")  // Multiple rooms
SseEvents.User("event-name", "user-id")     // Specific user
SseEvents.Roles("event-name", "admin")      // Users with roles

// Common presets
SseEvents.Common.Refresh
SseEvents.Common.Notification
SseEvents.Common.Toast
```

### 7.6 Presence (IRealtimePresence)

Single-node "who's in which room" tracking (package `Swap.Htmx.Realtime`, namespace `Swap.Htmx.Realtime`).

```csharp
// AUTO-REGISTERED by AddSseEventBridge() (1.7.0, via TryAddSingleton) — just inject IRealtimePresence.
builder.Services.AddSseEventBridge();
// Override only if you need a distributed impl — register it FIRST (TryAddSingleton, so yours wins):
// builder.Services.AddSingleton<IRealtimePresence, RedisRealtimePresence>();
```

```csharp
// Wire into the SSE connection lifecycle: Track on connect, UntrackAll on disconnect.
_presence.Track(conn.ConnectionId, room, User.Identity?.Name);
try { conn.JoinRoom(room); await conn.KeepAlive(cancellationToken: ct); }
finally { _presence.UntrackAll(conn.ConnectionId); }

_presence.Count(room);   // distinct connection count (not distinct users)
_presence.List(room);    // IReadOnlyList<PresenceEntry(ConnectionId, UserId?, Room)>
```

- `InMemoryRealtimePresence` is in-process only — NO cross-node backplane (per-node counts when scaled).
- Always `UntrackAll(connId)` on disconnect (a connection may be in several rooms). `Untrack(connId, room)` clears one room only.

================================================================================
## 8. Tag Helpers
================================================================================

### <swap-state>

Renders hidden fields for state container:

```html
<swap-state state="Model.State" />

<!-- Output -->
<div id="task-filter-state" class="swap-state-container">
    <input type="hidden" name="Status" value="all" />
    <input type="hidden" name="Page" value="1" />
</div>
```

### <swap-nav>

SPA-style navigation link:

```html
<!-- Instead of -->
<a href="/tasks" hx-get="/tasks" hx-target="#main-content" hx-push-url="true">Tasks</a>

<!-- Use -->
<swap-nav to="/tasks">Tasks</swap-nav>
<swap-nav to="/tasks" target="#sidebar">Tasks (sidebar)</swap-nav>
```

All Swap tag helpers require `@addTagHelper *, Swap.Htmx` in `_ViewImports.cshtml`.

### <swap-scripts>

Renders the client script block (htmx + Swap client, plus dev tools in Development). Put at the end of `<body>`.

```html
<body hx-ext="morph">
    <!-- morph=true adds idiomorph (§3.5); sse=true adds the htmx SSE extension (§7) -->
    <swap-scripts morph="true" sse="true" />
</body>
```

- Attributes: `morph` (bool), `sse` (bool), `devtools` (bool? — null = dev only), and overridable sources
  `htmx-src` / `idiomorph-src` / `sse-src` (vendor locally instead of the CDN default).
- REQUIRED for morphing (§3.5), the client OOB guards — `data-swap-seq` / `data-swap-hash` / `data-swap-if-exists` (§3.4), optimistic UI `data-swap-optimistic` (§3.8), and flash toasts (§3.6).

### <swap-frame>

Turbo-Frame-style region: lazily loads its own content and scopes navigation to itself.

```html
<swap-frame id="cart" src="/cart" loading="lazy">
    <div class="skeleton">Loading…</div>   <!-- shown until loaded -->
</swap-frame>
<!-- → <div id="cart" hx-get="/cart" hx-trigger="revealed once" hx-swap="innerHTML" hx-target="this"> -->
```

- `loading="lazy"` → `hx-trigger="revealed once"`; `eager` (default) → `load once`. Links/forms inside target the frame (`hx-target="this"`) unless you set `hx-target`. No `src` → plain identified container.

### <swap-upload>

File-upload `<form>` with a live `<progress>` bar (wired to htmx `xhr:progress`).

```html
<swap-upload name="file" url="/upload" target="#result" swap="innerHTML">
    <button type="submit">Upload</button>
</swap-upload>
```

- `name` and `url` are REQUIRED (missing either → renders nothing). `multiple` (bool) allows multi-file.
  Emits `hx-post` + `hx-encoding="multipart/form-data"` + a file input + progress bar. Handle with `IFormFile` server-side.

================================================================================
## 9. Configuration Options
================================================================================

### SwapHtmxOptions

```csharp
builder.Services.AddSwapHtmx(options =>
{
    // Auto-suppress layout for HTMX requests (recommended)
    options.AutoSuppressLayout = true;
    
    // Default target for <swap-nav>
    options.DefaultNavigationTarget = "#main-content";
    
    // Additional folders to search for partial views
    options.PartialViewSearchPaths.Add("Dashboard");
    options.PartialViewSearchPaths.Add("Tasks");
    
    // Register event configurations
    options.AddConfig<TaskEventConfig>();
    
    // Direct event bus configuration
    options.EventBus.When(...)
        .RefreshPartial(...);
    
    // Error handling
    options.ErrorHandling.Enabled = true;
    options.ErrorHandling.ErrorViewName = "_SwapErrorToast";
    options.ErrorHandling.ShowExceptionDetails = env.IsDevelopment();
    
    // Diagnostics (dev only)
    options.Diagnostics.EnableClientLogging = true;
    options.Diagnostics.EnableDevToolsPanel = true;
    options.Diagnostics.WarnOnUnhandledEvents = true;
    options.Diagnostics.WarnOnMissingOobTargets = true;
});
```

================================================================================
## 10. Testing (Swap.Testing)
================================================================================

### HtmxTestClient

```csharp
public class TaskTests : IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>
{
    private readonly HtmxTestClient<Program> _client;
    
    public TaskTests(WebApplicationFactory<Program> factory)
    {
        _client = new HtmxTestClient<Program>(factory);
    }
    
    [Fact]
    public async Task CreateTask_ReturnsOobSwaps()
    {
        var response = await _client
            .AsHtmxRequest()
            .HtmxPostAsync("/tasks", new Dictionary<string, string>
            {
                ["Title"] = "New Task",
                ["ProjectId"] = "1"
            });
        
        await response
            .AssertSuccess()
            .AssertContainsAsync("New Task")
            .AssertHasOobSwapAsync("task-list")
            .AssertHasOobSwapAsync("task-count")
            .AssertHasTriggerAsync("task.created");
    }
    
    [Fact]
    public async Task Index_ReturnsFullPageForBrowser()
    {
        // No HX-Request header = browser request
        var response = await _client.GetAsync("/tasks");
        
        await response
            .AssertSuccess()
            .AssertContainsAsync("<html")        // Full page with layout
            .AssertElementExistsAsync("#task-list");
    }
    
    [Fact]
    public async Task Index_ReturnsPartialForHtmx()
    {
        var response = await _client.HtmxGetAsync("/tasks");
        
        await response
            .AssertSuccess()
            .AssertDoesNotContainAsync("<html")  // Partial, no layout
            .AssertElementExistsAsync("#task-list");
    }
}
```

### HtmxTestResponse Assertions

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `.AssertStatus(code)` | Check HTTP status |
| `.AssertSuccess()` | Check 2xx status |
| `.AssertContainsAsync(text)` | Check response contains text |
| `.AssertDoesNotContainAsync(text)` | Check response doesn't contain text |
| `.AssertElementExistsAsync(selector)` | Check DOM element exists |
| `.AssertElementTextAsync(selector, text)` | Check element text content |
| `.AssertHasOobSwapAsync(targetId)` | Check OOB swap present |
| `.AssertHasTriggerAsync(eventName)` | Check HX-Trigger header |
| `.AssertHasHeaderAsync(name, value?)` | Check response header |

### Cookie Persistence

Cookies automatically persist across requests within the same `HtmxTestClient` instance:

```csharp
// Login sets session cookie — persists automatically
await _client.HtmxPostAsync("/auth/login", loginData);
var dashboard = await _client.GetAsync("/dashboard"); // authenticated

// Inspect cookies
var cookies = _client.Cookies.GetAllCookies();

// Clear for test isolation
_client.ClearCookies();
```

### OOB Swap Introspection

Inspect all out-of-band swaps:

```csharp
var response = await _client.HtmxPostAsync("/shop/purchase", data);

// Get all OOB swaps as structured data
var swaps = await response.GetOobSwapsAsync();
// Each OobSwap has: TargetId, SwapMode, HtmlContent

// Fluent assertions
await response
    .AssertOobSwapExistsAsync("cart-count")
    .AssertOobSwapContentAsync("cart-count", "5")
    .AssertOobSwapCountAsync(3);
```

### Trigger Payload Assertions

Deep-inspect HX-Trigger JSON payloads:

```csharp
// Dot-path to nested values
response
    .AssertTriggerPayload("showToast", "message", "Created")
    .AssertTriggerPayload("itemCreated", "data.id", "123")
    .AssertTriggerCount(2);

// Typed deserialization
var toast = response.GetTriggerPayload<ToastData>("showToast");
```

### Form Field Assertions

```csharp
await response
    .AssertFormFieldExistsAsync("Email")
    .AssertFormValueAsync("Name", "John")
    .AssertFormValueAsync("Active", "true")   // checkbox
    .AssertFormValueAsync("Status", "active"); // select
```

### Snapshot Scrubbers

```csharp
SnapshotManager.ScrubUrls();                            // Replace URLs → [URL]
SnapshotManager.ScrubRegex(@"tenant-\w+", "[TENANT]"); // Custom pattern
SnapshotManager.ClearScrubbers();                       // Reset
```

================================================================================
## 11. Complete Examples
================================================================================

### 11.1 CRUD with Events (Full Pattern)

```csharp
// Events/TaskEvents.cs
[SwapEventSource]
public partial class TaskEvents
{
    public const string TaskCreated = "task.created";
    public const string TaskUpdated = "task.updated";
    public const string TaskDeleted = "task.deleted";
}

// Events/TaskEventConfig.cs
public class TaskEventConfig : ISwapEventConfiguration
{
    public void Configure(SwapEventBusOptions config)
    {
        config.When(TaskEvents.Task.Created)
            .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, ctx =>
            {
                var db = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>();
                return db.Tasks.OrderByDescending(t => t.CreatedAt).ToList();
            })
            .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.TaskCount, SwapViews.Tasks._Count, ctx =>
            {
                var db = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>();
                return db.Tasks.Count();
            })
            .Toast("Task created!", ToastType.Success);

        config.When(TaskEvents.Task.Deleted)
            .RefreshPartial(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, ctx =>
            {
                var db = ctx.RequestServices.GetRequiredService<AppDbContext>();
                return db.Tasks.OrderByDescending(t => t.CreatedAt).ToList();
            })
            .Toast("Task deleted", ToastType.Warning);
    }
}

// Controllers/TasksController.cs
public class TasksController : SwapController
{
    private readonly AppDbContext _db;
    
    public TasksController(AppDbContext db) => _db = db;
    
    [HttpGet("/tasks")]
    public IActionResult Index([FromSwapState] TaskFilterState state)
    {
        var query = _db.Tasks.AsQueryable();
        
        if (state.Status != "all")
            query = query.Where(t => t.Status == state.Status);
        
        if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(state.Search))
            query = query.Where(t => t.Title.Contains(state.Search));
        
        var tasks = query
            .OrderByDescending(t => t.CreatedAt)
            .Skip((state.Page - 1) * 10)
            .Take(10)
            .ToList();
        
        return SwapView(new TaskListViewModel { Tasks = tasks, State = state });
    }
    
    [HttpPost("/tasks")]
    public async Task<IActionResult> Create(CreateTaskInput input)
    {
        var task = new Task { Title = input.Title, Status = "pending" };
        _db.Tasks.Add(task);
        await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
        
        return (await SwapEventAsync(TaskEvents.Task.Created))
            .WithClientAction("closeModal")
            .Build();
    }
    
    [HttpDelete("/tasks/{id}")]
    public async Task<IActionResult> Delete(int id)
    {
        var task = await _db.Tasks.FindAsync(id);
        if (task != null)
        {
            _db.Tasks.Remove(task);
            await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
        }
        
        return SwapEvent(TaskEvents.Task.Deleted).Build();
    }
}
```

### 11.2 Filter + Pagination View

```html
@model TaskListViewModel
@{ var state = Model.State; }

<!-- State container -->
<swap-state state="state" />

<div class="filters">
    <!-- Status filter -->
    @foreach (var status in new[] { "all", "pending", "completed" })
    {
        <button class="@(state.Status == status ? "active" : "")"
                hx-get="/tasks?Status=@status&Page=1"
                hx-target="#task-content"
                hx-include="#task-filter-state">
            @status
        </button>
    }
    
    <!-- Search -->
    <input type="text" 
           name="Search"
           value="@state.Search"
           placeholder="Search tasks..."
           hx-get="/tasks?Page=1"
           hx-target="#task-content"
           hx-include="#task-filter-state"
           hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms" />
</div>

<div id="task-content">
    <!-- Task list -->
    <div id="task-list">
        @foreach (var task in Model.Tasks)
        {
            <div id="task-row-@task.Id" class="task-row">
                <span>@task.Title</span>
                <button hx-delete="/tasks/@task.Id"
                        hx-confirm="Delete this task?">
                    Delete
                </button>
            </div>
        }
    </div>
    
    <!-- Pagination -->
    <div class="pagination">
        @for (int i = 1; i <= Model.TotalPages; i++)
        {
            <button hx-get="/tasks?Page=@i"
                    hx-target="#task-content"
                    hx-include="#task-filter-state"
                    class="@(state.Page == i ? "active" : "")">
                @i
            </button>
        }
    </div>
</div>

<!-- Task count (updated via OOB) -->
<div id="task-count">
    @Model.Tasks.Count tasks
</div>
```

================================================================================
## 12. Debug Checklist
================================================================================

| Problem | Check |
|---------|-------|
| Wrong HTML returned? | Ensure action returns `SwapView()` not `View()` |
| Nothing updated? | Confirm element IDs match (`SwapElements.*`) |
| Partial not found? | Add folder to `PartialViewSearchPaths` |
| State not persisting? | Ensure `<swap-state>` exists and `hx-include` targets it |
| Events not firing? | Ensure `.WithTrigger(EventKey)` or event chain configured |
| Realtime not updating? | Ensure `AddSseEventBridge()` + `UseSseEventBridge()` present |
| SSE not connecting? | Check `sse-connect` URL and `sse-swap` event names match |

### Enable Generated Code Inspection

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EmitCompilerGeneratedFiles>true</EmitCompilerGeneratedFiles>
  <CompilerGeneratedFilesOutputPath>obj\Generated</CompilerGeneratedFilesOutputPath>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Remove="obj\Generated\**\*.cs" />
</ItemGroup>
```

================================================================================
## 13. Do This / Don't Do This
================================================================================

### DO

```csharp
// Use SwapView (auto-detects HTMX)
return SwapView(model);

// Use generated constants
return SwapView(SwapViews.Tasks.Index, model);
builder.AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.TaskList, SwapViews.Tasks._List, tasks);
return SwapEvent(TaskEvents.Task.Created);

// Multi-target response
return SwapResponse()
    .WithView(SwapViews.Tasks._Row, task)
    .AlsoUpdate(SwapElements.TaskCount, SwapViews.Tasks._Count, count)
    .WithCreatedToast("Task")
    .Build();

// State with URL override
<button hx-get="/tasks?Status=completed&Page=1"
        hx-include="#task-filter-state">

// Debounced search
<input hx-get="/search" 
       hx-trigger="keyup changed delay:300ms"
       hx-include="#state">

// Event-driven updates
return SwapEvent(TaskEvents.Task.Created, payload)
    .WithClientAction("closeModal")
    .Build();

// Modular event configuration
options.AddConfig<TaskEventConfig>();
```

### DON'T

```csharp
// DON'T: Returns full layout for HTMX requests
return View(model);

// DON'T: Magic strings
return SwapView("_TaskList", model);
builder.AlsoUpdate("task-list", "_TaskList", tasks);

// DON'T: Redirect breaks HTMX
return Redirect("/tasks");

// DON'T: Missing hx-include
<button hx-get="/filter">Filter</button>

// DON'T: Complex objects in state
public List<Task> Tasks { get; set; }  // Keep state flat

// DON'T: Inline event config when it gets complex
options.EventBus.When(Event1).RefreshPartial(...);
options.EventBus.When(Event2).RefreshPartial(...);
// Use ISwapEventConfiguration instead
```

================================================================================
## API Quick Reference
================================================================================

### SwapController Methods

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `SwapView(model)` | Auto partial/full based on request |
| `SwapView(viewName, model)` | Explicit view name |
| `SwapResponse()` | Start fluent builder |
| `SwapEvent(key, payload?)` | Fire event (sync model factories) |
| `SwapEventAsync(key, payload?)` | Fire event (async model factories) |
| `RenderPartialToStringAsync(view, model)` | Render partial for SSE |

### SwapRealtimeController Methods

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `ServerSentEvents(handler)` | Create SSE endpoint |

### SwapState Members

| Member | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `ContainerId` | Auto-generated element ID |
| `Protected` | Enable encryption (override) |
| `UrlSync` | Sync to query string (override) |
| `[SwapUnprotected]` | Exempt field from encryption |
| `[FromSwapState]` | Bind state from request |

### Event Chain Methods

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `.When(EventKey)` | Start event chain |
| `.RefreshPartial(id, view, modelFactory)` | OOB partial refresh |
| `.RefreshPartialAsync(id, view, asyncFactory)` | Async OOB refresh |
| `.Toast(message, type)` | Add toast notification |
| `.ChainToSse(eventKey, sseEventKey)` | Forward to SSE |
